purpose: Enrich team-agent draft simulator with signals that are NOT analyst picks or consensus ranks. Includes late-breaking visits, medical updates, insider-only trade rumors, and coach/GM tendency profiles that inform scheme-fit and behavioral weights.
agent_note: Per model philosophy: analyst content feeds team PROFILES only, never picks directly. Entries here should map to profile weights (scheme-fit, GM archetype, trade propensity), not to pick overrides.

private_visits_since_april_17._note: Top-30 visit window closed April 15, but late private workouts and final-week meetings continue. Entries below are confirmed with date >= 2026-04-17 or reported in post-4/17 articles. Most tracker entries lack exact dates; we include only those we can attribute.

ALL_TEAMS_JORDYN_TYSON_WORKOUT.event: private workout open to all NFL teams, ~25 routes, 1 drop
ALL_TEAMS_JORDYN_TYSON_WORKOUT.significance: Teams now comfortable with hamstring/injury history; Matt Miller reports floor 5, ceiling 16.

visit_tracker_multi_team_signals.significance: Three-team TE-needy pattern implies Day 2 consensus; all three teams have legitimate TE demand.
PIT visit reinforces McCarthy QB-whisperer positioning; ARI is trade-down leverage or fallback.
Confirmed Day 2 QB cluster; IND interest aligns with Richardson trade rumor.
DAL hosting Downs while simultaneously rumored trading up for Styles is notable — suggests multiple Ohio State defender pathways.
Confirms Veach public comment about bringing in nearly every draftable edge.

Jermod McCoy.detail: Ran 4.38 at Tennessee pro day 2026-03-31; medically cleared mid-season 2025 but declined return. 'No limits' at pro day per McCoy. Right ACL suffered Jan 2025.
Jermod McCoy.profile_weight_note: Consistent with current model's 0.50 medical multiplier being appropriate — cleared but teams still discount for 2025 lost season and lack of 2025 tape.

Jordyn Tyson.detail: Held all-teams workout 2026-04-17 at ~25 routes, 1 drop. Did not complete combine or ASU pro day due to hamstring. Matt Miller (ESPN): 'conversation starts at 5, off board no later than 16.' ACL/MCL/PCL history from 2022 Colorado season plus hamstring reaggravation remain in file.
Jordyn Tyson.profile_weight_note: Existing reduction (per repo feedback 'Tyson already reduced') still defensible; the 4/17 workout limits further slide but does not move him back to top-10 consensus on durability alone.

Zachariah Branch.detail: Arrested 2026-04-19 in Athens GA on two misdemeanors (obstruction of sidewalk, obstruction of law enforcement). Booked 1:26a, released 3:44a on $39 bond. Projected Round 2 pre-incident.
Zachariah Branch.profile_weight_note: Character-concern multiplier should apply for teams with known character sensitivity (PIT, BAL, NE post-Vrabel). Four days pre-draft with no formal team statements yet — do NOT drop him off boards entirely; apply modest slide (half to full round).

methodology_notes: Agent-based sims gain accuracy from EXPLICIT scheme-fit weights per team (e.g., KC applies +bonus to press-man CBs because Spagnuolo runs 30.8% man, top-10 in NFL). Without this, agents default to positional need and miss archetype mismatches.

Visit coverage is an ACCESS signal, not a pick signal. A player on 5+ visit lists has wide-market interest; a player visiting only 1-2 teams may have a specific landing spot. Weight this as demand distribution, not team preference override.

GM trade frequency buckets (5.2+/draft = active, 1.4-/draft = conservative per NFL IQ data) should drive each agent's trade-propensity prior. CLE/KC/LAR/MIN/PHI/NO should fire trade branches more often; LAC/CIN/BAL rarely.

Completed trades (NYG-CIN Lawrence, DEN-MIA Waddle) change both draft order AND team need profiles. A simulator that doesn't re-propagate need vectors after trades will produce stale picks — this is a more impactful bug than analyst-divergence on any single player.

Late-breaking character flags (Branch arrest 4/19) should apply a team-specific multiplier based on each team's historical character-sensitivity (PIT, NE, BAL high; LAR, SF low). Uniform penalty across all teams is wrong — it flattens the agent population.

Medical clearances that happen AFTER the Combine (Tyson 4/17 workout, McCoy 3/31 pro day) reduce variance but do not reverse the prior injury discount. The correct adjustment is to NARROW each player's distribution, not to raise its mean to pre-injury consensus.

When analyst consensus heavily agrees (Kiper + McShay + Brugler all on DAL-trade-up-for-Styles), that is a team-PROFILE signal — the market has decoded DAL's need + McClay preferences. This should firm DAL's defense-first agent prior, NOT cause the sim to hard-code the Styles pick.

Independent model divergence from consensus is diagnostic: if our team-agent picks a different player than consensus for the same team, the question is whether our PROFILE is wrong (fix it) or whether consensus is groupthink (verify against primary sources). Per user feedback in MEMORY.md: 'divergence from consensus is a quality failure' — but the fix is always richer profiles, never loosening independence.
high_impact_findings_summary: NYG-CIN Lawrence trade (4/18-19, insider-confirmed) gives NYG picks 5 AND 10 — cascades through top-10 ordering; CIN is OUT of R1 entirely. Most impactful data point in this batch.

DAL trade-up to 6 (Rapoport + triple-consensus of Kiper/McShay/Brugler on Sonny Styles) is the most-telegraphed R1 move; DAL agent needs defense-first + LB/EDGE prior weighted higher than mock-normal.
ARI exploring trade-down from 3 with Jeremiyah Love as fallback (Schefter). If no trade partner, Love-to-ARI at 3 becomes base case — this should raise RB-top-3 probability materially.
Branch arrest 4/19 at Athens is a live character red-flag 4 days pre-draft; apply team-sensitivity-weighted slide, not uniform drop.
Schefter market-read: FEWER R1 trades than recent years (more teams want down than up). Trade-propensity parameter should be damped this cycle across the simulator.
